response:
The internet traces its origins to 1960s U.S. Department of Defense computer networking research, with precursor network ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969, and the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols established the technical foundation for the modern internet.
Major milestones that expanded access and use included Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web, the 1993 launch of the public-facing Mosaic browser, the late 1990s dot-com boom, and the subsequent rise of cloud computing, social media, and ubiquitous mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone release.
While most speculative internet companies failed in the 2001 dot-com crash, survivors including Amazon and Google grew into some of the most valuable businesses in history.
Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people globally and underpins nearly every aspect of modern life.
